title.en: This is ridiculous! Unbelievable! 정말 황당하네! 어이가 없네!

description.en: When something is completely absurd and ridiculous! Learn strong negative reactions like "Eo-i-ga eop-ne" and "Jang-nan-hae?".
description.ko: 너무 어처구니없고 황당한 일을 겪었을 때! "어이가 없네"와 "장난해?" 등 강한 부정의 리액션을 배워봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 리액션 장인이 되기 위해 모이셨군요? 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! Are you all gathered here to become reaction masters today? I'm Mina.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I just got a parking ticket. But I was only parked for 30 seconds to pick up my coffee!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네~? 겨우 30초요?
content.translation: What~? Only 30 seconds?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와..., 진짜 "어이가 없네~!"
content.translation: Wow..., that's so ridiculous!

insight.title: 어이가 없네
insight.description: To be at a loss for words because something is so absurd
insight.usage_tip: Used when a situation is unfair or unbelievable.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Right? 30 seconds! "This is ridiculous!"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 "황당하네요!"
content.translation: That's really absurd!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I know. I tried to explain. but the officer didn't listen.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "어이가 없네". It's a very famous line from a Korean movie. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 유아인 배우의 유명한 대사죠.
content.translation: Correct! It's a famous line by actor Yoo Ah-in.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 상대방의 행동이 상식 밖일 때. "어이가 없네" 라고 하면 딱이에요.
content.translation: When someone's behavior is beyond common sense. 'Eo-i-ga eop-ne' is perfect.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "어이가 없네...,"
content.translation: This is ridiculous...,

content.speaker: John
content.text: And what about when I feel like someone is mocking me.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 인상을 살짝 쓰면서. "지금 장난해요?" 라고 물어보세요.
content.translation: In that case. Frown a little and ask. 'Ji-geum jang-nan-hae-yo?'.

insight.title: 장난해?
insight.usage_tip: Use this when you are offended or think someone is messing with you.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "지금..., 장난해?"
content.translation: Are you kidding me right now?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오~ 존 씨. 방금 표정 아주 리얼했어요!
content.translation: Oh~ John. Your expression just now was so real!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine we ordered a large pizza. but when the box opened. there was only one tiny slice inside.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어?
content.translation: Huh?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "이게 뭐야~!" 존. 피자가 왜 이래요?
content.translation: What is this~! John. Why is the pizza like this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh. I think the delivery guy was hungry.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와..., 진짜..., "말도 안 되는 소리" 하지 마세요!
content.translation: Wow..., seriously. don't talk such nonsense!

insight.title: 말도 안 되는 소리
insight.usage_tip: Use this when someone says something logically impossible or stupid.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Maybe it's a new diet pizza.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "지금 장난해요?" 배고파 죽겠단 말이에요!
content.translation: Are you kidding me right now? I'm starving!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm kidding. I'm kidding! The real pizza is right here.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아~..., "황당하네" 정말!
content.translation: Ah~ that's so absurd. honestly!

content.speaker: John
content.text: These reactions are so powerful when you're frustrated.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 너무 어이없어서 말이 안 나올 때. 오늘 배운 표현들을 꼭 써보세요!
content.translation: Right. When you're so speechless from absurdity. Make sure to use the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five "ridiculous" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "어이가 없네~!"
content.translation: This is ridiculous!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "정말 황당하네!"
content.translation: That's so absurd!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "말도 안 되는 소리야!"
content.translation: That's nonsense!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "지금 장난해?"
content.translation: Are you kidding me?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "어처구니가 없네!"
content.translation: I'm at a loss for words!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분 주변에는 황당한 일보다는 즐거운 일만 가득하길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope your surroundings are filled with joyful things rather than absurd ones!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. We'll be back with another "Dae-bak" episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.meaning: To be ridiculous. To be speechless
vocabulary.example: 그 사람 정말 어이가 없네요. (That person is really ridiculous.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be absurd. To be nonsensical
vocabulary.example: 황당한 꿈을 꿨어요. (I had an absurd dream.)
지금 장난하는 거 아니죠? (You're not joking right now. are you?)
겨우 이거밖에 없어요? (Is this all there is?)
오늘 저녁은 피자를 먹읍시다. (Let's eat pizza for dinner.)
그건 상식적으로 이해가 안 돼요. (That doesn't make sense in terms of common sense.)
